title: Kim Jong-un Opens Memorial Museum for DPRK Soldiers Killed in Ukraine
detail: Kim Jong-un inaugurated the 'Memorial Museum of Combat Feats at the Overseas Military Operations' in Pyongyang, dedicated to North Korean soldiers killed fighting alongside Russian forces in Kursk Oblast, Ukraine. In his speech, Kim publicly praised soldiers who died by suicide rather than be captured by Ukrainian forces, calling them 'heroes' — the first time Kim explicitly acknowledged the scale of DPRK combat deaths and the extreme circumstances faced by DPRK troops in Ukraine. Russian State Duma Speaker Vyacheslav Volodin attended the ceremony and thanked Kim for DPRK's role in 'the liberation of Kursk.' South Korean intelligence estimates approximately 15,000 DPRK troops were deployed to Kursk, with approximately 2,000 killed in combat. The museum opening marks a decisive shift from previous DPRK denials of troop deployment.

media.caption: Kim Jong-un at the inauguration of the memorial museum honoring DPRK soldiers killed in Ukraine

title: Russia-DPRK Sign Framework for Long-Term Military Cooperation 2027–2031
detail: Russian Defense Minister Andrei Belousov traveled to Pyongyang and met with Kim Jong-un and North Korean Defense Minister No Kwang Chol. Both sides agreed to place military cooperation 'on a stable, long-term footing' and announced readiness to sign a formal Russian-Korean Military Cooperation Plan covering 2027–2031. The framework formalizes the alliance forged through DPRK troop deployments to Russia and arms transfers including artillery shells and KN-23 ballistic missiles. The agreement marks the most comprehensive institutionalization of Russia-DPRK military ties since the June 2024 Comprehensive Strategic Partnership Treaty signed in Pyongyang. Western analysts described the plan as unprecedented in scope, encompassing joint training, weapons development cooperation, and intelligence sharing.

media.caption: Russian Defense Minister Belousov and DPRK Defense Minister No Kwang Chol agree on 2027-2031 military cooperation plan
